item.title: Trump official signals turnaround on blocked wind and solar projects
item.description: Interior secretary Doug Burgum agrees to resume project approvals to jumpstart permitting reform talks but remains vague on court ruling

item.title: Deals of the Week: Iberdrola and Orsted seal $6.5bn of sales key to strategies
item.description: Trio have been involved in major agreements that will shape their roles in renewables ion networks

item.title: TotalEnergies and Jera Nex BP-backed plan would slow German offshore wind growth
item.description: TotalEnergies offshore wind chief claims benefits for network operators, supply chain and taxpayers if proposals to lessen wake impacts are adopted

item.title: Wake plan backed by TotalEnergies and Jera Nex BP could delay Germany’s offshore wind target by 12 years
item.description: TotalEnergies offshore wind chief claims benefits for network operators, supply chain and taxpayers if proposals to lessen wake impacts are adopted

item.title: CIP acquires Orsted’s European onshore wind business, launches Perigus Energy
item.description: All-Denmark deal sees Orsted refocus on core offshore wind markets while CIP expands into burgeoning onshore renewables

item.title: Equinor exec who led oil giant's UK wind power push departs
item.description: Executive had led the oil and gas giant’s UK renewable energy push, including to build one of the world’s biggest offshore wind farms

item.title: Three EU member states face fines for failing to adopt renewable energy targets
item.description: The European Commission has taken Greece, Malta and Portugal to the EU's Court of Justice after missing the deadline to transpose updated renewable energy rules into national law.

item.title: EDPR chief warns over barriers to PPAs
item.description: Transcation costs and taxes are obstacles to electrification, says renewables giant's Europe CEO

item.title: EDP chief warns over barriers to PPAs
item.description: Transcation costs and taxes are obstacles to electrification, says renewables giant's Europe CEO

item.title: CIP warns Europe has wrong market conditions for grid expansion
item.description: Europe needs around €120bn in total grid investment to meet its electrification goals, but public funds alone won't be enough

item.title: Siemens Gamesa offshore CEO on why there is a 'dark cloud' over sector
item.description: Manufacturers still need to fill factories that cost hundreds of millions of euros in investments, wind turbine maker's offshore chief warns at WindEurope conference in Madrid

item.title: Us or them! Vestas exec hints at link between UK factory plan and Mingyang ban
item.description: Vestas executive says that competition from contenders who 'do not play the rules' can affect investment decisions – as anger grows in China about UK 'politicisation' of the wind turbines issue

item.title: Vestas exec hints at link between UK factory plan and Mingyang ban
item.description: Vestas executive says that competition from contenders who 'do not play the rules' can affect investment decisions – as anger grows in China about UK 'politicisation' of the wind turbines issue

item.title: Size matters: US wind rebound driven by dominant players
item.description: NextEra, AEP and Berkshire Hathaway accounted for more than half of capacity additions last year as Trump policies favour permitting and supply chain scale

item.title: US investment giant swoops for stake in European wind developer
item.description: Danish wind and solar developer Eurowind Energy says new investment will give it ‘financial strength’ to take it into its next phase

item.title: Iberdrola boss Galan quizzed over UK offshore wind auction and seabed plans
item.description: Spanish giant's executive chairman says planned wind farm that controversially missed out last time will enter AR8, but won't be drawn over more seabed

item.title: How a Danish wind group is upending orthodoxy to grow business
item.description: KK Group has enjoyed rapid growth by doing the ‘reverse’ of many other industrials in wind power sector, CEO tells Recharge

item.title: The Danish wind group upending orthodoxy to grow its business
item.description: KK Group has enjoyed rapid growth by doing the ‘reverse’ of many other industrials in wind power sector, CEO tells Recharge

item.title: Iberdrola wind output jumps – but grids drive profit growth
item.description: The renewables major reported a 42% rise in offshore wind output and continued heavy wind investment, but regulated networks delivered the stronger earnings growth and underpin its upgraded 2026 outlook

item.title: Refining giant seeks JV partner for tender-winning Baltic Sea offshore project
item.description: Poland’s Orlen also to commission country’s first offshore wind farm after the summer, company board member tells Recharge

item.title: Central European giant seeks partner for tender-winning Baltic Sea offshore project
item.description: Poland’s Orlen also to commission country’s first offshore wind farm after the summer, company board member tells Recharge

item.title: Wind developers lose millions in hidden costs by failing to price in community opposition
item.description: ‘Being 'green' does not automatically grant a social license to operate,’ report says

item.title: Massachusetts flips the switch on Vineyard Wind contracts
item.description: Power purchase agreements signed in 2018 finally activated for 'forever first' project mired in litigation

item.title: Offshore wind legacy deals move ahead in US market while new projects stall
item.description: Power purchase agreements signed in 2018 finally activated for 'forever first' project mired in litigation

item.title: Battery giants are signing deals – but the latest energy storage tech still has to prove its business case
item.description: Chinese battery giants are increasingly adding sodium-ion cells to their stack – but what can they actually deliver?

item.title: How Europe's biggest blackout helped turn role of renewables on its head
item.description: Some people blamed Spain's high renewables penetration for last year's blackout, but regulatory changes will now allow wind and solar to help stabilise voltage. Industry leaders say more can be done

item.title: Orsted chief: 'We can't keep building gold-plated offshore wind projects'
item.description: Standardisation crucial to driving down cost of renewable energy from offshore wind farms, says executive at Danish developer

item.title: CIP: We stayed out of ‘stupid auctions’ – and now offshore wind looks investable again
item.description: The investor is shifting across offshore wind, onshore wind, solar and batteries as COO Martin Neubert explains how local presence, being technology agnostic and having auctions that actually work are now decisive to returns

item.title: US offshore wind setback deepens as Ocean Winds takes refund deal
item.description: Joint venture of EDP and Engie in Total-pioneered refund-for-fossil trade on two early-stage projects, but no word on SouthCoast array

item.title: GE Vernova blade break halts wind farm using biggest onshore turbines
item.description: Probe underway after incident involving 6.1MW onshore machine at project built to fulfil Microsoft power deal
